title: Apple Notes Index

Search tips

Quick ways to find a specific note when you know roughly what you're looking for:

  • By filename in Obsidian: Cmd+O → type any part of the filename (date, slug words). The Quick Switcher will fuzzy-match across all notes in the vault.
  • Full-text search in Obsidian: Cmd+Shift+F → type any keyword. Obsidian searches the body of every .md file, so this finds matches inside the notes themselves.
  • From the terminal: grep -rli "<keyword>" ~/SecondBrain/raw/apple-notes/ — case-insensitive, lists matching files. Swap -rli for -rin to also see line numbers and matching content.
  • By year: Use the year jump links above, then Cmd+F within this page to scan titles.
